name: STOP-BANG
full_name: STOP-BANG Score for Obstructive Sleep Apnea
when_to_use: Patients presenting to a clinic setting with symptoms associated with OSA like: daytime drowsiness, snoring, observed periods of nighttime apnea, noisy breathing, etc.
when_not_to_use: Patient has already been diagnosed with OSA or uses CPAP at night
Not well-validated in the obstetrical population

items.label: snore
items.description: Do you snore loudly? (Louder than talking or loud enough to be heard through closed doors)

items.label: tired
items.description: Do you often feel tired, fatigued, or sleepy during the daytime?

items.label: observed
items.description: Has anyone observed you stop breathing during sleep?

items.label: pressure
items.description: Do you have (or are you being treated for) high blood pressure?

formula: snore + tired + observed + pressure + bmi + age + neck + gender

references.citation: Chung F, Yegneswaran B, Liao P, Chung SA, Vairavanathan S, Islam S, Khajehdehi A, Shapiro CM. STOP questionnaire: a tool to screen patients for obstructive sleep apnea. Anesthesiology. 2008 May;108(5):812-21.

notes: The STOP-BANG questionnaire screens for obstructive sleep apnea (OSA) only, not central sleep apnea. The first four items (STOP) are subjective questions asked to the patient; the last four (BANG) are objective measures. Patients screening as high-risk for OSA should have formal polysomnography for definitive diagnosis.
